title: FIX-467: Bug Creating First Library Ticket — Full Investigation & Fix Walkthrough
description: This tour walks through the complete lifecycle of FIX-467, a bug where creating the first library ticket in the staging environment produced no artifacts after several hours. The root cause was that the system was incorrectly using the user's authentication token instead of the built-in Helix system token when creating repositories — specifically for NetSuite file cabinet repos. Multiple agent runs were required to surface and fully resolve this issue. The tour follows the investigation in data-flow order: from initial user report, through agent triage runs, to the final corrected token-selection logic and verification.

steps.description: This is the entry point: the original ticket FIX-467 filed by the user. The user reported that creating a new library ticket in the staging environment produced no artifacts after several hours of waiting. Screenshots were attached as evidence of the failure. A critical constraint is noted here — the agent's available database is not an exact copy of production, but the fix must ensure the new library is created regardless. This ticket context file is the seed of the entire investigation and sets the scope: the problem is environment-specific (staging) and involves the artifact creation pipeline.
steps.title: Step 1: Original Ticket Report (FIX-467)

steps.description: In this second run, the Helix agent acknowledged the ticket and began triage. The discussion section records the first agent response at 2026-05-15T00:13:39Z. No code changes were landed yet — this run was focused on understanding the failure mode from the attached screenshots and the available (non-exact) database state. Data flow at this stage: user report → agent acknowledgment → environment inspection. The agent is scoping the problem before making any changes, which is the correct first step when the reported environment (staging) differs from the agent's available data.
steps.title: Step 2: Agent Triage Begins (Run 2)

steps.description: This run marks the first attempt at a fix — the agent reported 'Your changes are ready! Updated 1 repository.' and attached verification screenshots. However, this run only addressed a surface symptom, not the true root cause. The user responded (line 38+) clarifying that the system was still broken and pointing to the actual problem: for NetSuite file cabinet repos, the code was using the user's authentication token instead of the built-in Helix system token that should be responsible for repository creation. This is the pivotal moment in the investigation — the user's clarification redirected the agent toward the real token-selection logic bug.
steps.title: Step 3: First Fix Attempt & User Clarification (Run 3)

steps.description: This run context file captures the full discussion history up to and including the agent's understanding of the true root cause. The agent confirms: the previous fix addressed a symptom but not the underlying token-selection logic. For NetSuite file cabinet repo creation, the system must use the built-in Helix system token, not the user's personal token. This run is where the corrected mental model is established and the real code change is planned. Data flow: user clarification → agent root cause analysis → identification of the token-selection branch that needs to be fixed.
steps.title: Step 4: Root Cause Identified — Token Selection Logic (Run 4)

steps.description: This is the first CodeTour walkthrough artifact generated by the agent during run 4. It documents the investigation steps as understood at that point in the fix process. Note that this walkthrough was superseded by the one generated in run 5 (cmp7n46rs0046ks0uj44y27a3), which reflects a more complete and accurate understanding of the fix. Comparing the two walkthrough artifacts reveals how the agent's understanding of the root cause evolved between runs — a useful audit trail for future maintainers.
steps.title: Step 5: Intermediate Walkthrough Artifact (Run 4)

steps.description: This is the final, authoritative CodeTour walkthrough artifact generated in run 5. It reflects the complete and corrected understanding of the fix: the system must use the built-in Helix system token (not the user's token) when creating repositories for NetSuite file cabinet repos. The walkthrough here provides a cleaner narrative arc — from initial report through root cause identification to verified fix — and supersedes the earlier version from run 4. This file is the canonical reference for future developers investigating the token-selection logic in the library creation pipeline.
steps.title: Step 6: Final Authoritative Walkthrough (Run 5)

steps.description: This is the most complete ticket context file, representing the final run (cmp7tgh62007hks0u9n9j7gb1) that contains the full discussion history. It confirms the entire investigation chain: initial report → surface fix attempt → user correction → root cause identification → final fix. The discussion thread here is the authoritative audit log for FIX-467. Future maintainers can use this file to understand why the token-selection logic was changed, what the original misunderstanding was, and why NetSuite file cabinet repo creation requires the Helix system token rather than delegating to the user's credentials. The major code paths for library creation are now clean: system token is used for repo creation, user token is scoped only to user-facing operations.
steps.title: Step 7: Final Run Context — Full Audit Trail & Summary
